Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x3000 "ADRENALIN-3000" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Adrenalin.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
The following steps were taken to only display the intro once and not attempt to reload it from disk between plays:
1. Both the intro and game PRGs from the original disk were fully decrunched 2. Then for the game PRG: a) removed the calls to the kernal load routine ($FFD5) at $4029 and the kernal save routine ($FFD8) at $6448 to remove disk access from the game (for high score loading and saving) b) the JMP $6294 instruction at $6190 was changed to JMP $CF00 c) the onscreen message displayed on game over was modified at $6EE2 to remove the reference to reloading the intro by pressing fire d) a new routine was placed at $CF00 which resets the SID registers, calls the SID tune init routine at $8000 and then sets $4313 to $21 (this is needed for the music to play properly), before jumping to $4000 to start a new game. e) it was then crunched with TSCrunch (https://csdb.dk/release/?id=216394) 3. And for the intro PRG: a) the code for loading the game PRG from disk at $9000 was removed b) the JMP $9000 instruction at $30D7 was changed to JMP $8200 c) a new routine was placed at $8200 which resets machine state and then copies a copy routine to the stack and executes it. This routine copies the crunched game PRG from $A000 back down to $0801 and starts it with the BASIC RUN command (the start address for the game itself is $4000). 5. A new PRG (the dumpfile) was created by placing the uncrunched intro at $0801 and the newly crunched game at $C000
